speakers.name: Amit Jhingran
direct_answers: We will definitely be much better than 10%-11% if you look at the individual quarter. But overall guidance for the year remains at 14%.
dodged_or_vague: We are not going to speak to the particular product margin.
speakers.summary: Amit Jhingran, the Managing Director and CEO of SBI Life Insurance, delivered a confident and comprehensive overview of the company's performance for Q3 FY26. He highlighted the company's strong financial results, including surpassing INR 5 trillion in assets under management and leading the private life insurance segment with significant market share gains. Jhingran emphasized the impact of regulatory measures and the shift towards protection-oriented products, as well as the company's focus on innovation and digital transformation. He confidently addressed growth expectations, maintaining guidance for the year while acknowledging the evolving customer needs and market conditions.

speakers.name: Sangramjit Sarangi
speakers.summary: Sangramjit Sarangi, the President and CFO, provided a neutral and factual update on the financial metrics of SBI Life Insurance. He discussed the impact of GST and labor law changes on the company's profit after tax, highlighting a 4% growth. Sarangi assured stakeholders of the company's strong solvency position, well above regulatory requirements. His responses were clear and direct, focusing on the financial health and stability of the company.

speakers.name: Abhijit Gulanikar
direct_answers: We are working on that and we will launch that.
speakers.summary: Abhijit Gulanikar, President of Business Strategy, conveyed an optimistic outlook on SBI Life's strategic initiatives. He discussed the company's efforts in revamping its product suite, particularly focusing on annuity and protection products. Gulanikar highlighted the significant demand in the par portfolio and assured stakeholders of upcoming product launches. His responses were forward-looking, emphasizing the company's proactive approach to market trends and regulatory changes.

speakers.name: Subhendu Bal
speakers.summary: Subhendu Bal, the President and Chief Risk Officer, provided a cautious perspective on SBI Life's risk management and regulatory compliance. He emphasized the company's strong solvency ratio and readiness for potential regulatory changes. Bal's remarks reflected a focus on maintaining financial stability and cost efficiency, ensuring the company remains well-prepared for future challenges.

speakers.name: Smita Verma

She thanked participants for their engagement and directed them to the investor relations team for further inquiries. Verma's tone was neutral, emphasizing the company's openness to continued dialogue with investors.
